candidate_check.description: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux operating system must be configured so that the cron.allow file, if it exists, is group-owned by root.

check_content_excerpt:
Verify that the "cron.allow" file is group-owned by root.

Check the group owner of the "cron.allow" file with the following command:

# ls -al /etc/cron.allow
-rw------- 1 root root 6 Mar  5  2011 /etc/cron.allow

If the "cron.allow" file exists and has a group owner other than root, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Set the group owner on the "/etc/cron.allow" file to root with the following command:

# chgrp root /etc/cron.allow
title: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux operating system must be configured so that the cron.allow file, if it exists, is group-owned by root.
